list.title: APT: Action Expert Pretraining Improves Instruction Generalization of Vision-Language-Action Policies

extra.desc: Researchers address poor generalization in Vision-Language-Action models by proposing APT, a two-stage training method that pretrains action experts using vision-action pairs before integrating language conditioning to improve out-of-distribution instruction performance.

list.title: AlloSpatial: Agentic Harness Framework for Spatial Reasoning in Foundation Models

extra.desc: AlloSpatial framework enhances spatial reasoning in foundation models by converting egocentric observations into structured allocentric representations and enabling reliable spatial cognition through cognitive mapping and tool-use reasoning.

list.title: ActiveMimic: Egocentric Video Pretraining with Active Perception

extra.desc: ActiveMimic pretraining framework recovers camera and wrist trajectories from egocentric video to enable active perception learning that matches robot data performance.

list.title: WaveDiT: Distribution-Aware Wavelet Flow Matching for Efficient 3D Brain MRI Synthesis

extra.desc: WaveDiT generates full-resolution 3D brain MRIs using conditional flow matching in wavelet coefficient space with adaptive precision modeling, enabling efficient synthesis on standard hardware.

list.title: P3D-Bench: Benchmarking MLLMs for Parametric 3D Generation and Structural Reasoning

extra.desc: Parametric 3D generation benchmark evaluates models on geometric precision, semantic alignment, and assembly consistency through code-based modeling tasks.

list.title: AdaSR: Adaptive Streaming Reasoning with Hierarchical Relative Policy Optimization

extra.desc: An adaptive streaming reasoning framework enables models to dynamically allocate computation and reason during input streaming while maintaining task performance and reducing latency.

list.title: Measuring Epistemic Resilience of LLMs Under Misleading Medical Context

extra.desc: Large language models demonstrate reduced medical reasoning accuracy when exposed to misleading context, highlighting a critical gap in current evaluation methods that fails to assess epistemic resilience under adversarial conditions.

list.title: Rethinking RAG in Long Videos: What to Retrieve and How to Use It?

extra.desc: VideoRAG systems are extended to handle long egocentric videos with multi-modal retrieval across temporal granularities, addressing limitations in existing benchmarks and methods through a new benchmark and chunk-adaptive reranking approach.

list.title: CARVE: Certified Affordable Repair of Vetoed Maneuvers via Envelopes for Interactive Driving

extra.desc: Interactive driving scenarios reveal failures in autonomous vehicle rule systems where small adjustments could restore feasibility, leading to the introduction of CARVE, a certification framework that provides runtime proofs for multi-agent repairs without requiring predictions of other drivers' compliance.

list.title: Smaller Models are Natural Explorers for Policy-Level Diversity in GRPO

extra.desc: Small-to-Large Policy Optimization framework uses smaller models as natural explorers to enhance policy diversity and improve large language model training efficiency.

list.title: Benchmarking AI Agents for Addressing Scientific Challenges Across Scales

extra.desc: SciAgentArena presents a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating AI agents in real scientific research scenarios, revealing current limitations in novel insight generation and open-ended problem solving while identifying opportunities for improving agent reliability and autonomy.

list.title: APPO: Agentic Procedural Policy Optimization

extra.desc: Agentic Reinforcement Learning method that improves multi-turn tool-use capabilities by refining branching decisions and credit assignment through fine-grained decision points and procedure-level advantage scaling.

list.title: RhymeFlow: Training-Free Acceleration for Video Generation with Asynchronous Denoising Flow Scheduling

extra.desc: RhymeFlow accelerates diffusion transformers for video generation by decoupling denoising trajectories across frames, using keyframe anchoring and latent trajectory projection to maintain visual quality while reducing computational overhead.

list.title: RedAct: Redacting Agent Capability Traces for Procedural Skill Protection

extra.desc: Users rely on execution traces to observe agent behavior, diagnose failures, and ensure accountability. These traces contain rich procedural detail, including tool invocations, intermediate decisions, and error-recovery logic. Yet this detail can expose private procedural skills, allowing downstream methods to recover key formulas, thresholds, and strategies

list.title: MBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark on Memory Capability for Video World Models

extra.desc: A new benchmark called MBench is introduced to evaluate the memory capabilities of video world models, focusing on entity, environment, and causal consistency over extended temporal horizons.

list.title: OmniDirector: General Multi-Shot Camera Cloning without Cross-Paired Data

extra.desc: A unified framework for camera motion cloning that uses grid motion videos as representation and integrates multimodal diffusion transformers for enhanced video generation control.

list.title: RepFusion: Leveraging Multimodal Priors for Denoising in Representation Space

extra.desc: RepFusion leverages multimodal large language models as noisy representation encoders for diffusion transformers in text-to-image generation, outperforming traditional approaches that train new denoisers.

list.title: LLM Agents Can See Code Repositories

extra.desc: Visual repository representations enhance LLM-based coding agents by improving structural understanding and reducing token consumption during issue resolution.

list.title: Orchestra-o1: Omnimodal Agent Orchestration

extra.desc: An omnimodal agent orchestration framework is presented that enables efficient collaboration across multiple modalities through unified task decomposition and specialized sub-agent execution, achieving superior performance on complex multimodal benchmarks.

list.title: Avatar V: Scaling Video-Reference Avatar Video Generation

extra.desc: Avatar V is a production-scale framework that generates behaviorally recognizable avatar videos by conditioning on full video references through sparse attention mechanisms and motion representation streams.

list.title: Hy-Embodied-0.5-VLA: From Vision-Language-Action Models to a Real-World Robot Learning Stack

extra.desc: HyVLA-0.5 is an end-to-end robotic learning system that integrates data collection, model design, pre-training, fine-tuning, and reinforcement learning for real-world deployment.

list.title: HarnessX: A Composable, Adaptive, and Evolvable Agent Harness Foundry

extra.desc: HarnessX enables adaptive and evolvable AI agent runtime interfaces through compositional primitives, trace-driven evolution, and feedback loops that improve both harness design and model training.

list.title: μ_0: A Scalable 3D Interaction-Trace World Model

extra.desc: A scalable world model called μ₀ uses 3D traces to predict smooth trajectories for key interaction points, enabling embodiment-agnostic robot learning without action labels.

list.title: Memory is Reconstructed, Not Retrieved: Graph Memory for LLM Agents

extra.desc: MRAgent combines associative memory graphs with active reconstruction to enable dynamic memory access during reasoning, improving long-horizon memory reasoning while reducing computational costs.

list.title: ClinHallu: A Benchmark for Diagnosing Stage-Wise Hallucinations in Medical MLLM Reasoning

extra.desc: ClinHallu is a benchmark for diagnosing and mitigating hallucinations in medical multimodal large language models through stage-wise reasoning analysis and trace-supervised fine-tuning.

list.title: The Hidden Power of Scaling Factor in LoRA Optimization

extra.desc: Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) scaling factor α functions as a primary optimization driver rather than a secondary learning rate complement, with theoretical and empirical analysis revealing its superior impact on convergence and optimal scaling behavior.

list.title: Skip a Layer or Loop It? Learning Program-of-Layers in LLMs

extra.desc: Pretrained language models can execute layers dynamically through flexible program-of-layers strategies that improve accuracy while reducing computational overhead compared to standard fixed-depth inference.

list.title: Dense Supervision, Sparse Updates: On the Sparsity and Geometry of On-Policy Distillation

extra.desc: On-policy distillation exhibits sparse parameter updates that are distributed across layers and favor FFN components, while maintaining geometric properties distinct from standard dense parameter rewriting.

list.title: From AGI to ASI

extra.desc: Artificial general intelligence development may lead to artificial general superintelligence through multiple pathways, requiring interdisciplinary global preparation for transformative societal changes.

list.title: From Chatbot to Digital Colleague: The Paradigm Shift Toward Persistent Autonomous AI

extra.desc: Large Language Models are evolving from conversational systems to integrated AI colleagues with enhanced reasoning capabilities and persistent work environments.

list.title: OmniVideo-100K: A Dataset for Audio-Visual Reasoning through Structured Scripts and Evidence Chains

extra.desc: An automated audio-visual question answering system uses entity-anchored video scripting and clue-guided QA generation to improve cross-modal reasoning and temporal consistency in video analysis.

list.title: When is Your LLM Steerable?

extra.desc: Activation steering effectiveness can be predicted from early decoding states using a GBDT classifier, enabling efficient steering strength optimization with reduced computational cost.

list.title: VISTA: View-Consistent Self-Verified Training for GUI Grounding

extra.desc: VISTA is a GRPO-based training framework for GUI grounding that uses multiple consistent views of the same GUI instance to improve training stability and accuracy.

list.title: On the Limits of LLM Adaptability: Impact of Model-Internalized Priors on Annotation Task Performance

extra.desc: Large language models exhibit limited ability to correct zero-shot errors through prompting, with model performance more strongly linked to definition-specific familiarity than text-level memorization metrics.

list.title: Rethinking Psychometric Evaluation of LLMs: When and Why Self-Reports Predict Behavior

extra.desc: Psychometric assessments of LLM behavior reveal that specific behavioral frameworks like Theory of Planned Behavior show better coherence with actual responses than broad personality traits, particularly within shared conversations.

list.title: See What I See, Know What I Think: Dense Latent Communication Across Heterogeneous Agents

extra.desc: Heterogeneous multi-agent systems can effectively transfer knowledge through aligned KV-cache communication, achieving better performance than text-based methods with reduced computational costs.

list.title: Getting Better at Working With You: Compiling User Corrections into Runtime Enforcement for Coding Agents

extra.desc: TRACE is a skill-layer pipeline that mines user corrections to create runtime checks, significantly reducing preference violations in interactive LLM agents.

list.title: WebChallenger: A Reliable and Efficient Generalist Web Agent

extra.desc: WebChallenger presents a web agent framework that improves autonomous navigation through structured page representation and cognitive-inspired mechanisms, achieving high performance with open-weight models.

list.title: The Cold-Start Safety Gap in LLM Agents

list.title: Risk Under Pressure: Compute-Aware Evaluation of Adversarial Robustness in Language Models

extra.desc: Compute-aware evaluation framework using FLOPs and risk-compute curves reveals non-monotonic effects of alignment training and varying attack costs across different harm categories.

list.title: ToolSense: A Diagnostic Framework for Auditing Parametric Tool Knowledge in LLMs

extra.desc: Parametric tool retrieval models show reduced performance and understanding when evaluated with realistic ambiguous queries compared to standard benchmarks, revealing a dissociation between knowledge retrieval and true tool comprehension.

list.title: A Stationary (and Therefore Compatible) Representation is All You Need

extra.desc: Stationary representations learned through d-Simplex fixed classifiers ensure model compatibility during sequential fine-tuning and updates, enabling continuous retrieval services without reprocessing.

list.title: ArogyaSutra: A Multi-Agent Framework for Multimodal Medical Reasoning in Indic Languages

list.title: Where, What, Why, and Importance: Structured Defect Grounding for Text-to-Image Feedback

list.title: HYDRA-X: Native Unified Multimodal Models with Holistic Visual Tokenizers

list.title: TreeSeeker: Tree-Structured Trial, Error, and Return in Deep Search

extra.desc: TreeSeeker is an inference-time framework that uses tree-structured search with branch-and-return control to manage exploration and exploitation in deep search tasks, improving performance through systematic trial-and-error decision making.

list.title: Flash-GMM: A Memory-Efficient Kernel for Scalable Soft Clustering
